_notes: Per-operation perf budgets, used by tests/test_perf_regression.py.
Each entry pairs a description with a budget_ms ceiling.
The test takes BEST-OF-N (default 5) samples and asserts the best
sample beats the budget. Best-of-N is intentional: under parallel
test-suite load + GC noise, p50/p99 are unreliable, but 'is the
code CAPABLE of meeting the budget' is a real regression signal.
Decrease (tighten) a budget when code legitimately gets faster.
Increase (loosen) ONLY with a written justification in the same PR
Baselines were measured 2026-05-17 on WSL2 Ubuntu 24.04 / Python 3.12
with the suite already warm. Ceilings carry 3-5x headroom over the
measured best-of-5 so machine-to-machine variance doesn't trip the

scan_restriction_sites_puc19.description: _scan_restriction_sites on a pUC19-size (2686 bp) random sequence

build_seq_text_puc19.description: _build_seq_text on pUC19-size (2686 bp) with 10 features, cold cache per call

build_seq_text_20kb.description: _build_seq_text on 20 kb with 80 features, cold cache per call
